title: rubus × evagatifrons
intro:
Rubus × evagatifrons, also known as the black raspberry, is a hybrid plant derived from the cross between Rubus idaeus (red raspberry) and Rubus occidentalis (black raspberry), resulting in a unique combination of characteristics from both parent species.

This plant is rich in antioxidants and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues and inflammation.

The black raspberry is known for its various health benefits, including its potential to prevent cancer, reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease, and provide anti-inflammatory effects.

The herbal preparations of Rubus × evagatifrons include jams, jellies, and teas, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
